feedback.text: As many others in this feedback section, I would like if video games were also covered by this act more thoroughly. As it stands, many video games require a persistent connection to a proprietary server, one that may be shut down with no notice and compensation for owners of the game. This can be used to force players to move to a new entry in the game series and spend more money. The European Citizen's Initiative "Stop Destroying Videogames" already has over a million signatures, showing widespread support for this stance.
